mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid monthly increase in the unemployment rate signals immediate labor market deterioration, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances when UNRATE rises > 0.2pp monthly.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns, with interaction term coefficient statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in the industrial production index reflects robust manufacturing and supply-side capacity, which translates to higher corporate profit margins and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns during early-cycle expansions, or the signal reverses direction when inflation exceeds 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation remains elevated above 315 while the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0%, real discount rates rise sharply, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX yields positive 5-day returns in > 60% of observations when both CPIAUCSL exceeds 315 and FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates a strengthening labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rolling Rank IC for PAYEMS growth remains below 0.01 over 3-year windows, or 60-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with payroll changes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and retail sales validates sustained aggregate demand, reducing recession probabilities and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction effect between GDPC1 and RSAFS becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in predicting 20-day returns, or positive joint signals yield negative mean returns.

mechanism_narrative: A two-month consecutive decline in industrial production signals immediate supply-side contraction and weakening economic activity, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances following two consecutive monthly declines in INDPRO.
